workflowConfig.name: Contract Comparison
workflowConfig.description: Compare sequential versions of a contract using deterministic paragraph-level text diffing to identify all additions, deletions, and modifications across the negotiation chain, producing a structured DOCX report with clause amendment frequency analysis

files.description: File objects with fileId and fileName, ordered chronologically (oldest first). Minimum 2 required.

config.name: Contract Difference Analyzer
config.systemPrompt: You are an expert legal document analyst specializing in contract comparison. You receive structured diff output showing paragraph-level text differences between adjacent versions in a document sequence. Produce a comprehensive legal analysis with structured tables. Use precise legal terminology. Distinguish between substantive modifications (different legal effect) and rewordings (same legal effect, different language). Identify cross-reference renumbering separately from substantive changes. Use ### for clause type headings and #### for sub-sections. These are the only heading levels you may use. Some change items include an `inline_diff` field showing word-level changes with `[-deleted-]` and `{+added+}` markers, and a `clause_ref` field with the extracted section reference. Use these to inform your classification and description. Output valid JSON only.
config.userPrompt:
Analyze the changes from "{{files[loop.index].fileName}}" to "{{loop.item.fileName}}" (transition {{loop.index + 1}} of {{filesToCompare | length}} in the version sequence).

## Diff Statistics
- Modified paragraphs: {{steps.compare_documents.output.data.stats.modified}}
- Added paragraphs: {{steps.compare_documents.output.data.stats.added}}
- Deleted paragraphs: {{steps.compare_documents.output.data.stats.deleted}}
- Unchanged paragraphs: {{steps.compare_documents.output.data.stats.unchanged}}
- High divergence: {{steps.compare_documents.output.data.stats.highDivergence}}
- Truncated: {{steps.compare_documents.output.data.truncated}}

Note: If high divergence is true, the documents differ significantly (>70% changed). This often occurs when comparing a draft against a substantially expanded version. Still analyze all available changes thoroughly. If truncated is true, only the first 500 change items are shown.

## Change Blocks
{{steps.compare_documents.output.data.changeBlocks|string}}

## Output Requirements
Return a JSON object with exactly two fields:

1. "analysis": A markdown string containing your legal analysis. For each change block, produce:
   - **Clause Type** identification (e.g., Indemnification, Limitation of Liability, Representations)
   - A table with columns: Aspect | Classification | Description | Legal Significance
   - Classification must be exactly one of: Addition, Deletion, Modification, Rewording
     - Modification = different legal effect (substantive change)
     - Rewording = same legal effect, different language (cosmetic/style change)
   - If changes are purely cross-reference renumbering, classify as "Rewording" and note it
   - Keep descriptions to 1-2 sentences
   - Group changes under clause type headings. Within each group, list each change with its specific clause reference (from the `clause_ref` field) in the Description column
   - When an `inline_diff` field is available, reference the specific words that changed rather than describing the change generically

2. "clauses": An array of objects, each with:
   - "clauseType": string (the clause type you identified)
   - "classification": one of "Addition", "Deletion", "Modification", "Rewording"
   - "clauseRef": the clause/section reference if identified, or null
   - "versionFrom": "{{files[loop.index].fileName}}"
   - "versionTo": "{{loop.item.fileName}}"

This structured data will be used for amendment frequency analysis across all version transitions.

analysis.description: Markdown legal analysis with clause-by-clause tables

clauses.description: Structured clause classifications for frequency analysis

clauseType.description: The identified clause type (e.g., Indemnification, Limitation of Liability)

classification.description: One of: Addition, Deletion, Modification, Rewording

stepsConfig.name: Generate Report Overview with Frequency Analysis

config.name: Contract Comparison Overview
config.systemPrompt: You are an expert legal analyst. Generate a professional executive introduction for a contract version comparison report that includes amendment frequency analysis across the negotiation chain. Be concise and data-driven.
config.userPrompt:
Write a 3-4 paragraph executive introduction for a contract comparison report with the following metadata:

- Document versions (chronological): {{fileNames|join(' → ')}}
- Number of version transitions analyzed: {{filesToCompare|length}}
- Total clause classifications across all transitions: {{allClauses|length}}

## Clause Amendment Data
{{allClauses|string}}

The introduction should:
1. Identify all document versions in the negotiation sequence by name, in chronological order
2. Describe the scope and methodology (deterministic paragraph-level text comparison across sequential version pairs)
3. Analyze the clause amendment data to identify:
   - Which clause types were most frequently negotiated across version transitions
   - The distribution of change types (Addition vs Deletion vs Modification vs Rewording)
   - Evolution patterns: clauses that were modified in multiple consecutive transitions vs clauses that stabilized after early changes
   - Use the versionFrom/versionTo fields to identify which transitions affected each clause
4. Direct the reader to the detailed version-by-version analysis that follows

Keep it under 300 words. Be specific about which clauses were most frequently amended and in which transitions.
